_description: Litigation risk index — identifies which clauses are most frequently subject to Indian court disputes, the case law, and drafting fixes.

common_disputes: What constitutes 'Material Breach' — most litigated contractual concept in India
Adequacy of notice before termination
Wrongful termination — damages vs specific performance (SA 1963 S.14)
landmark_cases: ONGC v Saw Pipes Ltd (2003) — penalty clause enforced as reasonable estimate
General Assurance Society Ltd v Chandmull Jain — termination without breach
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Define 'Material Breach' precisely. List specific events that constitute material breach. Set clear cure periods.

common_disputes: Post-employment non-compete void under ICA S.27
Interim injunction applications in High Courts
Training bond recovery
landmark_cases: Niranjan Shankar Golikari v Century Spinning (SC 1967) — during-employment restraint valid
Percept D'Mark v Zaheer Khan (SC 2006) — post-employment restraint void
Homag India v Ulfat Rai (Delhi HC 2015) — narrowly drafted NCA upheld
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Limit to protection of Confidential Information. Define specific clients. Max 12-month duration post-employment. Link to CI, not general competition.

common_disputes: MSME payment delay — Samadhan portal complaints increasing
TDS deduction disputes — 194C vs 194J classification
GST non-compliance blocking input tax credit
landmark_cases: MSME Samadhan — over 1 lakh cases filed; compound interest (3x bank rate) is significant
Karnataka Electricity Board v IT Commissioner — TDS on professional fees
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Specify MSME status check. Expressly state applicable TDS section. Include GST-compliant invoice requirements. Add late payment interest.

common_disputes: Copyright Act S.17 vs S.19 — employee vs contractor ownership
Background IP vs foreground IP boundary
Open source contamination
landmark_cases: RG Anand v Delux Films (SC) — copyright in original literary work
Emergent Genetics v Shailendra Shivam — assignment must be written
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Express written assignment. Define Background/Foreground IP boundary. Address open source. Require contractor to obtain assignments from sub-contractors.

common_disputes: IBC vs SARFAESI race — parallel proceedings
Acceleration without notice
MAC clause triggering disputes
landmark_cases: Innoventive Industries v ICICI Bank (SC 2018) — IBC overrides RDB Act
Swiss Ribbons v UOI (SC 2019) — IBC constitutionality upheld
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Specify grace periods clearly. Define MAC trigger objectively. Add notice before acceleration. Ensure cross-default threshold is quantified.

common_disputes: COVID-19 as force majeure — Energy Watchdog v CERC (SC 2017) — economic hardship not FM
FM used to avoid payment — generally not permitted
Notice delay — failure to notify within prescribed period
landmark_cases: Energy Watchdog v Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (SC 2017) — non-availability of gas not FM
Standard Retail v G.S. Global Corp (Bombay HC 2020) — COVID lockdown may constitute FM
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Exhaustive list. Include pandemic expressly. Exclude payment obligations. Include notification window and mitigation obligation.

common_disputes: Eviction — tenant refuses to vacate
Wrongful eviction — self-help eviction by landlord
Notice adequacy — TPA S.106 minimum notice
landmark_cases: Ashoka Kumar Tandon v Virendra Kumar (SC) — oral eviction notice insufficient
Rent Control Act protections for controlled premises
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Comply with TPA S.106 notice periods. Do not use self-help eviction. Reference Rent Control Act if applicable. v3.0 fixed false-positive IRE trigger.

common_disputes: Wrongful termination without enquiry
Retrenchment without S.25F compensation
Gratuity forfeiture disputes
landmark_cases: Central Inland Water Transport Corporation v Brojo Nath Ganguly (SC 1986) — unconscionable employment termination
State Bank of India v Sundara Money (SC 1976) — workman definition
high_risk_clauses.drafting_fix: Mandatory domestic enquiry for misconduct dismissal. Provide written charge sheet. Ensure S.25F compliance for retrenchment. Never forfeit gratuity except for IDA misconduct grounds.

systematic_risk_fixes.issue: TDS non-compliance across all payment clauses
systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Payer faces interest under IT Act S.201(1A) + penalty under S.271C
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: Every payment clause must specify: (a) applicable TDS section; (b) rate; (c) obligation to file return; (d) obligation to issue TDS certificate

systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Recipient loses input tax credit; can be denied under CGST Act S.16(2)(b)
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: Payment clauses must require tax invoices with: GSTIN, HSN/SAC code, GST amount, place of supply, date

systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Compound interest at 3x RBI bank rate; Samadhan portal complaint; MSME facilitation council arbitration
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: All payment clauses must include MSME carve-out with 45-day payment timeline and compound interest provision

systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Jurisdictional challenge; ambiguity on Part I vs Part II of A&C Act; supervisory court uncertainty
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: Every arbitration clause must say: 'The seat of arbitration shall be [City], India'

systematic_risk_fixes.issue: IP not assigned in writing in service contracts
systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Client has no copyright in deliverables; contractor retains ownership under Copyright Act S.17 (unlike employment)
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: Service and consulting agreements must include written IP assignment signed by both parties

systematic_risk_fixes.issue: POSH Act ICC not referenced in employment contracts
systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Criminal liability for employer; Rs.50,000 fine per employee; licence cancellation
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: All employment agreements must reference POSH policy and ICC existence

systematic_risk_fixes.issue: DPDPA 2023 not addressed in data-processing agreements
systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Data Protection Board of India penalties up to Rs.250 crore for data fiduciaries
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: Any agreement involving personal data processing must add CORE_DPDPA_COMPLIANCE_001

systematic_risk_fixes.issue: Stamp duty not acknowledged in contracts
systematic_risk_fixes.impact: Unstamped instrument inadmissible in court under Indian Stamp Act S.35
systematic_risk_fixes.fix: CORE_IDENTITY_001 includes stamp duty acknowledgment; ensure agreement is executed on proper stamp paper
